observations.recommendation:
The Municipal Mayor require the concerned offices to submit the RIS, SSMI and Distribution List in support of the recorded expenses, to avoid disallowance in audit. 

Moreover, instruct the Municipal Accountant to set up the inventory accounts in the books for the purchase of goods and other supplies and record the corresponding expense accounts only upon issuance and submission of the SSMI/RIS.

Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the CY 2022 Quarterly Financial Statements, Monthly Trial Balance, and the quarterly schedule of Subsidiary Ledger Balances of the controlling accounts within the reglementary period.

The Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned officials to: immediately submit the DVs for November and December 2022; and

Review the disbursement process to address the reason/s for the delay in the submission of disbursement vouchers and other reports and henceforth submit the DVs pursuant to the deadline set by the RRSA.

The Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to address the delay in the recording of transactions so that the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements can be prepared and submitted on time.

Likewise, Management is enjoined to impose the sanction provided in Section 122(2) of PD 1445 to the concerned official/s for the failure to submit the required reports to the Audit Team.

The Municipal Mayor instruct the GSO to conduct the physical count of PPE and submit the RPCPPE on or before January 31 of each year. 

The RPCI be submitted not later than July 31 and January 31 each year for the first and second semesters.

Management, through the Municipal Accountant,  maintain the special accounts in the General Fund for each economic enterprise and the 20% DF. 

Moreover, the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the post-closing trial balances and financial statements of these economic enterprises to the Audit Team.

Management direct the responsible official to explain why the retention money was not deducted from the payment of progress billings of infrastructure projects. 

Henceforth, Management retain ten percent of progress payments or require the contractor to request the substitution of the retention money with irrevocable standby letters of credit from a commercial bank, bank guarantees or surety bonds pursuant to the provisions of RA 9184.

Management submit the contractor’s request/notice of extension and the report on the examination of facts and extent of delay to support the grant of the contract time extensions, otherwise, impose the total liquidated damages on the Contractor.

The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Treasurer and other concerned offices such as end-users prepare a holistic and comprehensive yet realistic catch-up plan to expedite the project implementation of these funded projects. 

Henceforth, include in the Municipality’s AIP and its proposed budget appropriation only those well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready projects of the Municipality.

Management provide a written explanation on charging the subject expenditures to LDRRMF notwithstanding the nature of such expenditures, which is not directly related to disaster risk reduction and management activities. 

Henceforth, Management properly assess the nature of the expenses to determine if these can be charged against the LDRRMF.

Management submit the lacking documents to support the suspension of work for these Infrastructure Projects to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.

Henceforth, Management ensure that the request and grant of work suspension during the implementation of an Infrastructure Project are duly supported with complete documentation as required under the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

The Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Assessor, facilitate the transfer of title of the unregistered parcels of land to establish absolute ownership and protect the legal interest of the Municipality over these lands.

The Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all the collections intact daily or not later than the next banking day in accordance with Section 32 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I, to avoid the risk of possible loss of government moneys.

The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the year-end financial statements, trial balances and bank reconciliation statements, otherwise withhold the salaries of the concerned personnel pursuant to Section 41 (3) of PD 1445.
Management submit the required supporting documents to substantiate the validity of the transaction.

The Municipal Accountant prepare a reconciliation report of the reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts on a timely basis and perform a constant review of the transactions to avoid errors and omissions.

Management secure the approval of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan from the National Solid Waste Management Commission and implement the Municipal’s Solid Waste Ordinance.

Management review and update every year the agency’s Solid Waste Management Plan by the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board as mandated under Section 16 of RA 9003.

The Municipal Government make concrete efforts by sending demand letters to NGOs/POs for the immediate settlement of their outstanding balances and improve the present monitoring and control measures on the liquidation of fund releases. 

For future grants of financial assistance to NGOs/POs, strict adherence to COA Circular No. 2007-001 is enjoined to ensure prompt liquidation thereof.

Immediate submission of Copy of Civil Service Commission (CSC)-Approved Plantilla, Eligibility under RA 1080, Contract of Services, Job Descriptions, Assignment Orders, Examination Reports, Personal Data Sheets and Schedules of Faculty Teaching Loads, otherwise, the payments of honoraria may be disallowed in audit and all those who authorized the payments may be held jointly and severally liable for the amounts disbursed.

Strict compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 in the payment of honorarium. The Municipal Accountant should see to it that all disbursement vouchers/payrolls are duly supported with complete documentation before payments are made.

Thorough revalidation of the listed ineligible beneficiaries. 

Should the revalidation prove that the listed persons are indeed ineligible, we also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer send demand letters to the 614 ineligible beneficiaries for the immediate refund of the illegally received subsidy totaling ₱3,684,000.00.
The responsible officials provide us with further reconciliation of the liquidation report. If a deficiency is warranted, refund the amount to DSWD and furnish us with a copy of the Official Receipt.

Management direct the MDRRMO for the submission of Inventory Reports of relief goods and medicines as of December 31, 2020, to the Municipal Accountant for preparation of adjusting journal entries. 

The concerned officials conduct periodic inventory taking and reconciliation of accounting and inventory records for accuracy in financial reporting.

Management establish a website to promote transparency through the widest dissemination of information on the use of the funds and to avoid non-compliance with future similar requirements. 

Submit copies of the written notice from the LCE when the said reports were posted to its website to government offices where the submission of the reports is required.
Management submit the required documents for proper accountability of fund transfers.

The Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous recording, reclassify all progress billings to the appropriate Construction in Progress accounts and all the completed and fully paid projects to proper PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

The Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting entries to reflect the correct account balances. We also recommended that retention money be deducted from the payments of progress billings by the contractor or required to post substitute guarantees.

Management prepare and submit the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances as of December 31, 2020, together with the necessary supporting documents and 

Henceforth, adhere to the provisions and reportorial requirements set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020 to ensure that aid and donations received by the Municipality are properly accounted for.

The municipal accountant issue Confirmation, and Demand Letters to the specific persons liable to immediately settle the cash advances either by returning the money advanced if unspent, or by presentation of required supporting documents.  

A separate filing system be made for the granting of cash advance and liquidation thereof.  Such supporting documents for the granting and liquidation of cash advances, including the proof of exhaustion of all remedies to collect should be submitted to the Auditor. Failure on their part to comply with the submission of the liquidations shall be a ground for disallowance in audit.

The Municipal Accountant immediately conduct a detailed review of the transactions affecting taxes withheld and remitted to the BIR to ascertain the correctness of the balances and provide the Audit Team with the reconciliation and the Alphabetical List of Payees. If warranted, facilitate the immediate remittance to the BIR of the unremitted taxes to avoid payment of penalties and surcharges for late payment.

Strict compliance with Republic Act Nos. 8424 and 10963, and Section 5 of BIR Revenue Regulation No. 11-2018 is hereby recommended.

Submission of the approved travel order in connection with the official travel. Failure to submit the said document shall be a ground for disallowance of the total claims. 

Consequently, upon presentation of the approved travel order, refund the excess claims. Adherence to the provisions of EO No. 77 is enjoined to avoid personal liability.

Immediate submission of the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents for proper accountability. Otherwise, the Municipal Mayor should suspend the payment of salaries of concerned officials pursuant to Section 122(2) of PD 1445.

Strict compliance with Section 100 of PD 1445, Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Government Accounting Manual is hereby recommended.

Immediate submission of the seventy-seven (77) disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 100 of P.D. 1445, Section 344 of R.A. 7160 and Sections 37 and 40 of the NGAS for LGUs. Failure of the management to submit the said documents may be a ground for the issuance of a Notice of Disallowance.

Management conduct an actual physical inventory of the toilet bowls under the other supplies and materials account and the breeding stocks and maintain inventory records in accordance with Volume I, Sections 124 and 114 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System. 

Likewise, make a thorough investigation on those recipients of the toilet bowls and breeding stocks if these were already distributed and make an adjustment duly supported to come up with the correct balance of these accounts as of year- end.

Management make a representation with the Bureau of Internal Revenue for the computation and settlement of any compensation tax liability for Sibonga Community College personnel. 

Henceforth, the management ensure that withholding taxes are deducted from the payment of compensation in accordance with the BIR revenue regulations.

Management immediately conduct an assessment and technical evaluation of the water system project to find out the reasons for the non-operation and to undertake the necessary remedial measures so that expected benefits due to intended recipients be timely delivered and to ensure that the amount spent on  the construction of the water system would not have been wasted.

In order to provide access and equitable distribution of opportunities for the citizens to acquire good quality education, strict compliance with Section 2 (d) Article V of CHED Memorandum Order No. 32 Series of 2006 and Civil Service Commission Memorandum Circulars No. 22, s. 2016 and No. 25, s.2017.

Creation/maintenance of separate books of accounts for Sibonga Community College for management to easily monitor the status and operations of the school not only to make the school self-sustaining but also to provide quality education to their students.

Work out a feasible plan in order for such real properties to be put to beneficial use. Additional income could have been earned had the Municipality considered selling or leasing said lots to the illegal settlers at a reasonable price, taking into consideration the current market values of these lots and the financial capacities, in cases where the occupants are indigents.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations and the Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate agreed to:
Prepare and maintain the required Property Cards and PPELC for each category of PPE;
Complete and submit RPCPPE and conduct reconciliation thereof; and
Coordinate to undertake the one-time cleansing of PPE to arrive at reliable PPE balances.
We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to create an Inventory Committee for the effective and efficient conduct of the physical inventory count.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer agreed to:
Reconcile their respective records and adjust accordingly the RPT and SET balances per the NGAS Manual for LGUs to reflect the correct balances of these accounts; and
Submit the results of the account reconciliation together with the (JEV) of the RPT and SET receivables established at the beginning of the year, a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year, list of delinquent real property accounts and the summary of the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/Taxpayer's index card for evaluation.
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile the reciprocal accounts and make the necessary adjustment/s to reflect the correct balances of these accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to transfer the unexpended/ unobligated balance of the CY 2023 LDRRM-MOOE and QRF at year-end to the Trust Fund to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide for the appropriate disclosures in the Notes to the Financial Statements on the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF consistent with Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to address the delay in the recording of transactions so that the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements can be prepared and submitted on time; and
Impose the sanction provided in Section 122(2) of P.D. 1445 to the concerned official/s for the failure to submit the required reports to the Audit Team.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit to the Audit Team the CY 2023 Quarterly Financial Statements, Monthly Trial Balance, and the quarterly schedule of Subsidiary Ledger Balances of the controlling accounts within the reglementary period.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and other concerned officials review the disbursement process to address the reason/s for the delay in the submission of disbursement vouchers and other reports. Likewise, we recommended the suspension of payment of salaries of the officials concerned for failure to submit the documents and reports under Section 122 of P.D. 1445.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to exhaust all possible courses of action to collect and demand liquidation of these cash advances including the withholding of the accountable officers’ salary until the liquidation of the subject cash advances pursuant to Section 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all DVs for CF cash advances with the complete required supporting documents be submitted to the Audit Team within seven (7) days from the release of the check pursuant to COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials agreed to:
Provide a written explanation for  charging the subject expenditures to LDRRMF notwithstanding the nature of such expenditures, which are not directly related to disaster risk reduction and management activities; and
Properly assess the nature of the expenses to determine if the same can be charged against the LDRRMF.

We recommended and the BAC Chairperson and other concerned officials agreed to retain (a) ten percent of progress payments or require the contractor to request the substitution of the retention money with irrevocable standby letters of credit from a commercial bank, bank guarantees or surety bonds for infrastructure projects; and  (b) at least one percent of every progress payment, or a special bank guarantee equivalent to at least one percent of the total contract price for procurement of goods, pursuant to the provisions of R.A. No. 9184.
We recommended and the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant, and other concerned officials agreed to conform to Section 6 of Annex E of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 on the proper application of retention money for progress payments of government projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit the report on the examination of facts and the extent of the delay to support the grant of the contract time extensions, otherwise, impose the total liquidated damages of ₱506,773.92 on the Contractor.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer agreed to submit the lacking disbursement vouchers (DVs) and supporting documents of the subject transactions for evaluation by the Audit Team. Likewise, we recommended that the documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular 2012-001 and the rules and regulations in the procurement of goods provided under R.A. 9184 be properly observed and attached to the respective DVs.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Maintain the special accounts in the General Fund for each economic enterprise and the 20% Development Fund; and
Prepare and submit the post-closing trial balances and financial statements of these economic enterprises to the Audit Team once the first recommendation is complied with.

The Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the complete set of financial statements and supporting schedules for the year ended December 31, 2022. 

Further, submit the lacking Statement of Management Responsibility (SMR), Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) and supporting schedules for the CY 2021 reports. Otherwise, appropriate sanction should be imposed on the responsible officials/ employees causing delay thereon pursuant to Section 122 (2) of PD 1445.

The Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize expenses in the period in which they are incurred and not when paid. 

Furthermore, we recommend the following proposed adjusting journal entry:
The Municipal Accountant make the following proposed adjusting journal entry:

The Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to demand the submission of a liquidation report on the subject cash advances, otherwise make the following proposed adjusting entry: 

Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant record the grant of cash advances to municipal officials and employees under the account Advances for Officers and Employees and require submission of a liquidation report before recognizing the related expenses in the municipal books.

Management direct the KALAHI-CIDSS MIAC to submit the lacking documents for evaluation by the Audit Team. 

Likewise, submit a justification for the inclusion of some municipal officials in the MIAC even if they are not required as per Section 2.2.1.1 of Revised Community Based Infrastructure Manual Volume I.

The Municipal Accountant agreed to make the following proposed adjusting journal entry.

Adv. to Officers and Employees – ₱195,784 (dr)
Traveling Expenses – Local – ₱22,284.00 (cr)
Training Expenses – ₱173,500.00 (cr)

Interest Expenses – ₱1,544,512.12 (dr)
Accountable Forms Expenses – ₱1,544,512.13 (cr)

Computer Software – ₱40,000.00 (dr)
Repairs and Maintenance – Machinery and Equipment – ₱40,000.00 (cr)

The Municipal Accountant agreed to reclassify the amounts granted under Donations to Due from LGUs account and demand liquidation reports to ascertain if disbursements were made in accordance with the purpose before taking up the adjustments in the books. 

Henceforth, ensure that documentary requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001 as amended by COA Circular No. 2017-002 shall be complied with before any funds shall be released by the Municipality to the barangays.

We recommended that the municipal mayor direct the municipal accountant to submit on time the CY 2019 year-end financial statements enumerated in IPSAS 1 with supporting schedules. We also recommended for strict compliance with COA Circular Nos. 2015-002 dated March 9, 2015 and 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010, otherwise withhold the salaries of the concerned personnel pursuant to Section 41 (3) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Comply with COA circulars pertaining to timeliness of the submission of Financial statements and corresponding supporting schedules.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to submit the year-end financial statements, trial balances and bank
reconciliation statements, otherwise withhold the salaries of the concerned
personnel pursuant to Section 41 (3) of PD 1445.

We recommended that there be thorough revalidation of the listed
ineligible beneficiaries. Should the revalidation proved that the listed persons are
indeed ineligible, we also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer send demand
letters to the 614 ineligible beneficiaries for the immediate refund of the illegally
received subsidy totaling ₱3,684,000.00.

We recommended that the responsible officials provide us further
reconciliation of the liquidation report. If deficiency is warranted, refund the
amount to DSWD and furnish us a copy of the Official Receipt.

We recommended that Management submit the required supporting
documents to substantiate the validity of the transaction.

We recommended that Management direct the MDRRMO for the
submission of Inventory Reports of relief goods and medicines as of December 31,
2020 to the municipal accountant for preparation of adjusting journal entries. We
also recommended for the concerned officials to conduct periodic inventory taking
and reconciliation of accounting and inventory records for accuracy on financial
reporting.

We recommended that the Management establish a website to promote
transparency through widest dissemination of information on the use of the funds
and to avoid non-compliance of future similar requirements. We also
recommended to submit copies of the written notice from the LCE when the said
reports were posted to its website to government offices where the submission of
the reports is required.

We recommended that Management submit the required documents for
proper accountability of fund transfers.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned
officials to ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and
each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from
execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to
ensure timely review and evaluation of the same, so that the Management could
be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.

We recommended that the municipal accountant prepare the adjusting
entries to reflect the correct account balances and submit the respective
Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment. Henceforth, observe the procedural
rules and the proper classifications in the accounting and recording of its supplies
and properties in accordance with the New Government Accounting System for
LGUs and International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) and other
similar issuances to avoid financial misstatements.

We recommended that the municipal accountant make the necessary
adjusting entries to correct the erroneous recording, reclassify all progress
billings to the appropriate Construction in Progress accounts and all the
completed and fully paid projects to proper PPE accounts pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the municipal accountant prepare the adjusting
entries to reflect the correct account balances. We also recommended that
retention money be deducted from the payments of progress billings of the
contractor or required them to post substitute guarantees.

We recommended that the Management prepare and submit the Report
on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF for CY 2020 and henceforth, comply
with the reportorial requirements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management prepare and submit the
Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances as of December
31, 2020 together with the necessary supporting documents and henceforth,
adhere to the provisions and reportorial requirements set forth in COA Circular
No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020 to ensure that aids and donations received by
the Municipality are properly accounted for.

We recommended that the municipal accountant issue Confirmation, and
Demand Letters to the specific persons liable to immediately settle the cash
advances either by returning the money advanced if unspent, or by presentation
of required supporting documents. We also recommended that a separate filing
system be made for the granting of cash advance and liquidation thereof. Such
supporting documents for the granting and liquidation of cash advances,
including the proof of exhaustion of all remedies to collect should be submitted to
the Auditor. Failure on their part to comply with the submission of the
liquidations shall be a ground for disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the municipal accountant immediately conduct a
detailed review on the transactions affecting taxes withheld and remitted to the
BIR to ascertain the correctness of the balances and provide the Audit Team with
the reconciliation and the Alphabetical List of Payees. If warranted, facilitate the
immediate remittance to the BIR of the unremitted taxes to avoid payment of
penalties and surcharges for late payment.

Strict compliance with Republic Act Nos. 8424 and 10963, and Section 5 of
BIR Revenue Regulation No. 11-2018 is hereby recommended.

We recommended for the submission of the approved travel order in
connection with the official travel. Failure to submit the said document shall be a
ground for disallowance of the total claims. Consequently, upon presentation of
the approved travel order, we also recommended for refund of the excess claims.
Adherence to the provisions of EO No. 77 is enjoined to avoid personal liability.

We recommended that Management conduct the physical count of its
properties and submit the required RPCPPE in compliance to Section 266 of the
Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for LGUs.

We required the immediate submission of the disbursement vouchers and
supporting documents for proper accountability. Otherwise, the Municipal
Mayor should suspend the payment of salaries of concerned officials pursuant to
Section 122(2) of PD 1445.

Strict compliance with Section 100 of PD 1445, Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular
No. 2009-006 and Government Accounting Manual is hereby recommended.

We recommended the immediate submission of the seventy-seven (77)
disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents to the Audit Team pursuant
to Section 100 of P.D. 1445, Section 344 of R.A. 7160 and Sections 37 and 40 of the
NGAS for LGUs. Failure of the management to submit the said documents shall
be a ground for the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended that the municipal treasurer submit a written
explanation on the non-presentation of cancelled checks and submit the original
copies of the aforementioned cancelled checks for evaluation by this office.
Adherence to the provisions of the New Government Accounting Systems Manual
and other prescribed regulations is enjoined.

We recommended for the immediate submission of the above-mentioned documents with complete data to prevent disallowance of the disbursements in audit and those who authorized the payments from being jointly and severally liable for the amounts disbursed. We also recommended that henceforth, the municipal officials refrain from approving disbursements unless with complete documentary requirements to avoid disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Submit all lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the submission of the approved travel order in connection with the official travel. Failure to submit the said order shall be a ground for disallowance of the total claims. We also recommended for the submission of certification from the head of agency concerned that the claim is absolutely necessary in the performance of an assignment pursuant to EO No. 77. Non-submission of the same shall be a ground for issuance of notice of suspension. Adherence to the provisions of EO No. 77 is enjoined to avoid personal liability.
observations.managementAction: Submit lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management refrain from spending personal and unnecessary disbursements and utilize the LGU’s limited funds and resources to projects, plans and programs that is beneficial to the majority of the constituents in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-003.
observations.managementAction: Management will discourage transactions like expenditures that will not benefit the majority of the disbursements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality make an information dissemination drive to the public regarding their ordinance in the “no segregation/no collection” policy to protect public health and environment. We further recommended that the management initiate and coordinate with the barangay officials in establishing MRF within the barangay that is functional and adhere to the rules and regulations in the establishment of MRF provided under Sections 32 and 33 of R.A. 9003
observations.managementAction: The information dissemination is ongoing and the proper coordination is being addressed accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all accounts in compliance with Section 74 of PD 1445 and Item 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-11. To facilitate the timely preparation of the BRS, we also recommended that the Municipality make representation with the depository banks to furnish them Bank Statements within 5 days after the end of each month as provided for in Item 3.0 of the same COA Circular.
observations.managementAction: Initial reconciliations of previous periods is already been done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality comply the existing regulations for the establishment of the LDDRM Office and fill up the mandatory positions required to perform the functions of research & planning and operations & warning.
observations.managementAction: Comply with the regulations regarding the establishment of LDRRM office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality conduct annual physical count of all its PPE especially those transferred to various elementary and secondary schools to properly monitor custodianship thereof. The concerned municipal officials should immediately inform the DepEd Regional Office No. 7 of all PPE transferred to teachers/officials for proper monitoring and accountability.
observations.managementAction: Comply with the regulations. Initial steps have been done to address accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that close monitoring on the submission of liquidation reports be undertaken by the Municipality. Concerned LGU Accountants should be reminded/required to immediately submit the Reports of Disbursements and to return unused balance of fund transfer so that corresponding expenditures can be recorded in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Closely monitor liquidation of cash advances to other LGUs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 relative to settlement of audit disallowance. Agency officials should strictly enforce settlement of long outstanding disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Comply with COA Circular No. 2009-006.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to intensify its tax collection drive for an increased real property/special education tax collection. The Municipality should avail of all legal remedies either by administrative action through levy on real property or judicial actions as authorized under Sections 256, 257 and 258 of Republic Act 7160.
observations.managementAction: Intensify tax collection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal accountant prepare a reconciliation report of the reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts on a timely basis and perform constant review of the transactions to avoid errors and omissions.
observations.managementAction: Perform timely reconciliation of reciprocal accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management ensure that the projects, programs and activities of the 20% development fund and 5% DRRM are implemented and optimized for the benefit of their constituents. We also recommended that the municipal officials exercise prudence in the use of its limited cash resources to finance all the intended projects of the municipal government.
observations.managementAction: Prepare a plan to implement the funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management conduct an actual physical inventory of the toilet bowls under the other supplies and materials account and the breeding stocks and maintain an inventory records in accordance with Volume I, Sections 124 and 114 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System. Likewise, make a thorough investigation on those recipients of the toilet bowls and breeding stocks if these were already distributed and make an adjustment duly supported to come up with correct balance of these accounts as of year-end.
observations.managementAction: Perform physical inventory and make proper adjusting entries to the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management secure the approval of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan from the National Solid Waste Management Commission and to implement the Municipal’s Solid Waste Ordinance. We also recommended that the management review and update every year the agency’s Solid Waste Management Plan by Municipal Solid Waste Management Board as mandated under Section 16 of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: The agency complied with the NSWMC requirements and the plan is now on "for approval status".

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government make concrete efforts by sending demand letters to NGOs/POs for the immediate settlement of their outstanding balances and improve the present monitoring and control measures on the liquidation of fund releases. For future grants of financial assistance to NGOs/POs, strict adherence to COA Circular No. 2007-001 is enjoined to ensure prompt liquidation thereof.
observations.managementAction: Increase monitoring of cash advances. Collection/demand letters had already been sent to the concerned NGOs but liquidation reports have not been submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality pay their payroll through the use of the ATMs to promote efficiency and proper accountability. Through this, loss of government funds while in transit and the misapplication of unused payroll fund may be prevented. Moreover, it prevents payments to non-existent and unauthorized persons as it will be the bank who will validate the identities/existence of their clients.
observations.managementAction: Apply ATM payroll system. Initial steps have been done to switch to ATM payroll.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that municipal lots are registered under the Torrens Title System to better protect the interest of the municipal government. Proper reconciliation between the records of the Municipal Accountant and the Assessor’s Office should also be done. We also recommended that the Assessor’s Office monitor regularly the status and the purpose of these lots.
observations.managementAction: Ensure the registration of municipal owned lots under the Torrens Title System. Initial steps with regards the registration are already being taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately conduct an assessment and technical evaluation of the water system project to find out the reasons for the non-operation and to undertake the necessary remedial measures so that expected benefits due to intended recipients be timely delivered and to ensure that the amount spent in the construction of the water system would not have been wasted.
observations.managementAction: Reassess and evaluate the project.

observations.recommendation: In order to provide access and equitable distribution of opportunities for the citizens to acquire good quality education we recommended strict compliance with Section 2 (d) Article V of CHED Memorandum Order No. 32 Series of 2006 and Civil Service Commission Memorandum Circulars No. 22, s. 2016 and No. 25, s.2017.
observations.managementAction: Comply with CHED memorandum on Community College.

observations.recommendation: We requested for the immediate submission of Copy of Civil Service Commission (CSC)-Approved Plantilla, Eligibility under RA 1080, Contract of Services, Job Descriptions, Assignment Orders, Examination Reports, Personal Data Sheets and Schedules of Faculty Teaching Loads, otherwise the payments of honoraria may be disallowed in audit and all those who authorized the payments may be held jointly and severally liable for the amounts disbursed. We recommended strict compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 in the payment of honorarium. The Municipal Accountant should see to it that all disbursement vouchers/payrolls are duly supported with complete documentation before payments are made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the municipal mayor direct the municipal
accountant to submit on time the CY 2019 year-end financial statements
enumerated in IPSAS 1 with supporting schedules. We also recommended for
strict compliance with COA Circular Nos. 2015-002 dated March 9, 2015 and
2010-001 dated March 2, 2010, otherwise withhold the salaries of the concerned
personnel pursuant to Section 41 (3) of PD 1445.

We recommended that the municipal accountant prepare the
appropriate adjusting entries to reflect the correct account balances and
henceforth, record the procurement of equipments and supply and materials to
asset accounts pursuant to paragraphs 14 and 17 of IPSAS 17and observe
adequate internal control in recording its issuance or disposition.

We recommended for the immediate submission of the above-mentioned
documents with complete data to prevent disallowance of the disbursements in
audit and those who authorized the payments from being jointly and severally
liable for the amounts disbursed.

We also recommended that henceforth, the municipal officials refrain
from approving disbursements unless with complete documentary requirements
to avoid disallowance.

We recommended that the municipal accountant prepare a reconciliation
report of the reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the
books of accounts on a timely basis and perform constant review of the
transactions to avoid errors and omissions.

We recommended that the management ensure that the projects,
programs and activities of the 20% development fund and 5% DRRM are
implemented and optimized for the benefit of their constituents. We also
recommended that the municipal officials exercise prudence in the use of its
limited cash resources to finance all the intended projects of the municipal
government.

We recommended that the management conduct an actual physical
inventory of the toilet bowls under the other supplies and materials account and
the breeding stocks and maintain an inventory records in accordance with
Volume I, Sections 124 and 114 of the Manual on the New Government
Accounting System. Likewise, make a thorough investigation on those recipients
of the toilet bowls and breeding stocks if these were already distributed and
make an adjustment duly supported to come up with correct balance of these
accounts as of year-end.

We recommended for the submission of the approved travel order in
connection with the official travel. Failure to submit the said order shall be a
ground for disallowance of the total claims.

We also recommended for the submission of certification from the head
of agency concerned that the claim is absolutely necessary in the performance of
an assignment pursuant to EO No. 77. Non-submission of the same shall be a
ground for issuance of notice of suspension. Adherence to the provisions of EO
No. 77 is enjoined to avoid personal liability.

We recommended that the management refrain from spending personal
and unnecessary disbursements and utilize the LGU’s limited funds and
resources to projects, plans and programs that is beneficial to the majority of the
constituents in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-003.

We recommended that the management secure the approval of the 10-
year Solid Waste Management Plan from the National Solid Waste Management
Commission and to implement the Municipal’s Solid Waste Ordinance.

We also recommended that the management review and update every
year the agency’s Solid Waste Management Plan by Municipal Solid Waste
Management Board as mandated under Section 16 of RA 9003.

We recommended that the municipality make an information
dissemination drive to the public regarding their ordinance in the “no
segregation/no collection” policy to protect public health and environment.
We further recommended that the management initiate and coordinate
with the barangay officials in establishing MRF within the barangay that is
functional and adhere to the rules and regulations in the establishment of MRF
provided under Sections 32 and 33 of R.A. 9003.

We recommended that the management make a representation with the
Bureau of Internal Revenue for the computation and settlement of any
compensation tax liability for the Sibonga Community College personnel. We
also recommended that henceforth, the management ensure that withholding
taxes are deducted from the payment of compensation in accordance with the
BIR revenue regulations.

We requested for the immediate submission of the lacking/required supporting documents for the disbursements to be allowed in audit.

      We recommend strict compliance with Section 4(6) of  PD  1445  in  the
payment of  salaries/wages/allowances/bonuses.   Henceforth,  the  Municipal
Accountant should see to it  that  all  disbursement  vouchers/payrolls  are
duly supported with complete documentation before payments are made.

We recommend that the Municipality pay their payroll through  the  use
of the ATMs to promote efficiency and proper accountability.  Through this,
loss of government funds while in transit and the misapplication  of  unused
payroll fund may be  prevented.  Moreover,  it  prevents  payments  to  non-
existent and unauthorized persons as it will be the bank who  will  validate
the identities/existence of their clients.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant cause  the  submission  of
the lacking documents and submit  a  valid  explanation  in  regard  to  the
deficiencies  observed  in  the  other   supporting   documents   to   avoid
disallowance in audit.

We recommend that the Sangguniang Bayan Secretary  prepare/submit  the
Minutes of the regular sessions/attendance sheets/actual work/attendance  of
the official vis-à-vis the  work  schedule  or  performance  standards.   SB
Members/Municipals Officials/Resource Persons who were  present  during  the
Regular/Special Sessions should have signed  such  Minutes  to  ensure  that
what is embodied in the Minutes is what was duly agreed upon.   Furthermore,
we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer see to it  that  all  disbursement
vouchers are duly supported with complete documentation before payments  are
made.


      We recommend  strict  compliance  with  the  aforementioned  laws  and
regulations in the disbursement of  government  funds  for  the  payment  of
honoraria and RATA.  The concerned municipal officials should  refrain  from
approving disbursements unless duly supported with  complete  documentation.
The Municipal Accountant and  Treasurer  should  see  to  it  that  all  the
documentary requirements are attached to the claim before payments are  made
to avoid suspension and disallowances.

We recommend that the Municipality comply the existing  regulations  for
the establishment of the LDDRM Office and fill up  the  mandatory  positions
required to perform the functions of research & planning  and  operations  &
warning.

We request for the immediate submission  of  the  required  supporting
documents  with  complete  data,  otherwise  the  disbursements   shall   be
suspended  and/or  disallowed  in  audit  and  shall  hold  all  those   who
authorized the payment  as  jointly  and  severally  liable  of  the  amount
disbursed.  The following documents are as follows:


    • Justification why Notice of Award/Notice to Proceed  were  not  posted
      and why other fields in the PhilGEPS website were left blank

    • Printed Copy of the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed as  uploaded
      in the PhilGEPS website, if there is any


      We recommend that the BAC Officials fill out the required  information
in the PhilGEPS website in order for the interested stakeholder to know  the
whole procurement process. Moreover, such should be  updated  in  accordance
with the stage in the procurement process,  so  that  Notice  of  Award  and
Notice to Proceed can be posted and updated accordingly,  thereby  promoting
transparency. Furthermore, we recommend  that  municipal  officials  refrain
from approving disbursements unless all  the  documentary  requirements  are
attached to the claim to avoid suspension and disallowances.

We recommend strict compliance with Section 12 of RA 10121 and Section
5.3 of the NDRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No.  2014-1  in  the
staffing of the Municipality’s LDRRM Office for  proper  accountability  and
in order  for  it  to  effectively  fulfill  and  achieve  its  mandate  and
objectives

We recommend that the Municipality appropriate in their Annual  Budget
one  percent  (1%)  of  its  IRA  for  the  implementation/strengthening  of
programs, projects and activities of their respective LCP.  Such  allocation
should be duly supported with specific practical and doable plans.

      Failure to implement the provision under Section 15 of RA 9344 will be
sanctioned under Section 62 of the said Act which provides that “any  person
who violates any provision of RA 9344 or any rule or regulation  promulgated
in accordance thereof shall, upon conviction for each act  or  omission,  be
punished by a fine of not less than Twenty Thousand Pesos  (P20,000.00)  but
not more than Fifty Thousand Pesos (P50,000.00) or  suffer  imprisonment  of
not less than eight (8) years but not more than ten  (10)  years,  or  both,
such fine and imprisonment at the discretion of the court, unless  a  higher
penalty is provided for in the Revised Penal Code or special  laws.  If  the
offender is a public officer or employee, he/she shall, in addition to  such
fine and/or imprisonment, be held administratively liable and  shall  suffer
the penalty of perpetual absolute disqualification”.

observations.recommendation:
We therefore recommend that:

Accountable officers with outstanding balances be required to immediately submit their liquidation report/documents pursuant to Section 4.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

The Accounting Section be required to record the liquidation documents submitted by the concerned Accountable Officers so that the corresponding expense accounts will be booked up.

Management should impose sanctions on Accountable Officers who fail to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period thru the withholding of their salaries as provided under Section 5.1.3 of the above Circular, especially those with unliquidated balances totaling more than P50,000.00 and those with cash advances which have remained outstanding for more than one (1) year.

Management should strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We reiterate our recommendation to create an Inventory Committee to immediately conduct actual physical inventory of the Municipality’s PPE in order to determine its existence and to render a report thereon, a copy of which must be submitted to the Auditor in compliance with Section 124 Volume 1 of New Government Accounting System (NGAS).  The Physical Inventory Report should be reconciled with accounting records.

Further, we wish to emphasize that under Sec. 122 of PD 1445, otherwise known as the State Audit Code of the Philippines, the failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit documents and reports (one is the physical inventory report) shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied thereto.

We recommend that close monitoring on the submission of liquidation reports be undertaken by the Municipality.  Concerned LGU Accountants should be reminded/required to immediately submit the Reports of Disbursements and to return unused balance of fund transfer so that corresponding expenditures can be recorded in the books of accounts.

We recommend that the Municipal Government make concrete efforts by sending demand letters to NGOs/POs for the immediate settlement of their outstanding balances and improve the present monitoring and control measures on the liquidation of fund releases.  For future grants of financial assistance to NGOs/POs, strict adherence to COA Circular No. 2007-001 is enjoined to ensure prompt liquidation thereof.

We recommend Management to intensify its tax collection drive for an increased real property/special education tax collection.  The Municipality should avail of all legal remedies either by administrative action through levy on real property or judicial actions as authorized under Sections 256, 257 and 258 of Republic Act 7160.

We recommend that the LGU and the DSWD comply with the terms of agreement stipulated in the MOA, specifically in undertaking continued capacity building support to the beneficiaries.

The DSWD and LGU should initiate/provide livelihood projects for the first batch of trainees using the procured sewing and edging machines.  Additional trainings should be conducted so that the skills learned by the first batch of participants be imparted to other CSO members within the municipality.

We recommend that Municipal Treasurer should be required to submit the duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names of the taxpayers and the tax due at the start of each year to the Municipal Accountant as basis of recording in the books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables in order to present reliable and accurate receivable account balances.  Further, management should install a system that could facilitate the preparation of certified list of taxpayers with unpaid taxes.

We recommend that the persons paid in the payrolls for the wage differential totaling P295,585.28 be made to refund the amount claimed. Otherwise, municipal officials and employees responsible for the payment thereof shall be considered personally liable for the illegal disbursements.  Henceforth, strict adherence to established accounting and auditing rules and regulations in the disbursement of government funds is enjoined.
The officials of the municipality should initiate the titling of the parcels of land owned by the LGU in order to validate the ownership thereof.
